subject: Science
chapter: Exploring Substances: Acidic, Basic and Neutral

activities_projects.title: Natural Indicator Paper
activities_projects.objective: Prepare and test turmeric paper as a natural indicator
steps: Mix turmeric with a little water to make a paste
Apply the paste on paper strips and let them dry
Put a drop of soap solution on the strip
Observe the color change to reddish-brown
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how natural indicators react with bases
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful preparation and observation of color change (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Kitchen Substance Testing
activities_projects.objective: Classify common household items into acidic, basic, or neutral
steps: Take small samples of each substance in separate bowls
Test each with the turmeric indicator
Record which ones change color and which do not
Categorize them in a table
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies acidic and basic substances in daily life
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification in the table (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Litmus Paper Demonstration
activities_projects.objective: Observe the effect of acids and bases on litmus paper
steps: Dip blue litmus into vinegar and observe the change
Dip red litmus into lime water and observe the change
Record the final colors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns the standard laboratory test for acids and bases
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in describing color transitions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The China Rose Indicator
activities_projects.objective: Create an indicator using China Rose (Hibiscus) petals
steps: Place petals in warm water until the water becomes colored
Filter the solution to use as an indicator
Add to an acid (lemon) and a base (detergent)
Note the dark pink (acid) and green (base) colors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Discovers different colors produced by organic indicators
activities_projects.evaluation: Extraction of indicator and correct color recording (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Neutralization in a Bottle
activities_projects.objective: Observe the reaction between an acid and a base
steps: Pour vinegar into a bottle
Put baking soda inside a balloon
Fix the balloon over the bottle neck and drop the soda in
Observe the fizzing and balloon inflation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the gas release during a neutralization reaction
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the reaction observed (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Acid Rain Model
activities_projects.objective: Understand how pollutants make water acidic
steps: Test distilled water with litmus (it should stay neutral)
Blow air (CO2) through a straw into the water for 2 minutes
Test the water again with blue litmus
Observe the slight acidic shift
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates carbon dioxide to the formation of weak acids
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical connection between breath and acidity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Identifying Neutral Substances
activities_projects.objective: Learn that not everything is acidic or basic
steps: Test each liquid with both red and blue litmus
Note that no color change occurs in either
Define these as neutral substances
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Defines neutral substances based on indicator tests
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of neutral samples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Safety Poster: Handling Bases
activities_projects.objective: Learn safety precautions for corrosive substances
steps: Research why we shouldn't touch strong bases with bare hands
Draw 'Do' and 'Don't' illustrations for the lab
Include a warning about the 'soapy' feel of bases
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Promotes lab safety and awareness of substance properties
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and accuracy of safety information (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Antacid Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Understand how bases treat acidity in the stomach
steps: Add indicator to vinegar
Slowly add crushed antacid
Watch the indicator change back to a neutral color
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding of the 'Acid + Base' remedy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Substance Detective Quiz
activities_projects.objective: Test knowledge of acids, bases, and salts
steps: Create 5 MCQ questions on natural indicators
Create 5 True/False questions on neutralization
Swap with a partner and grade each other
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Consolidates theoretical knowledge of the chapter
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of questions and correct answering (10 marks)
